DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT, PROOF OF SERVICE, POINTS AND AUTHORITIES FILED BY DEFENDANT WEISS & COMPANY, LLP HEARING SET FOR JAN-26-2011 AT 09:30 AM IN DEPT 302

LAW AND MOTION 302, EFENDANT WEISS & COMPANY, LLP'S DEMURRER TO FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT. ARGUED AND THE COURT MODIFIED ITS RULING AS FOLLOWS: DEFENDANT'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AND 2ND CAUSES OF ACTION ARE SUSTAINED WITH LEAVE TO AMEND TO PLEAD WITH MORE SPECIFICITY WHERE DUTY LIES OR SPECIFIC FACTS OF NEGLIGENCE. DEFENDANT'S DEMURRER TO THE 3RD CAUSE OF ACTION IS SUSTAINED WITH LEAVE TO AMEND TO PLEAD SPECIFIC FACTS SHOWING WHETHER THE CONTRACT WAS WRITTEN, ORAL OR IMPLIED CONDUCT PER CCP 430.10(G). DEFENDANT'S DEMURRER TO THE 4TH CAUSE OF ACTION IS SUSTAINED WITH LEAVE TO AMEND TO ESTABLISH THAT DEFENDANT HAD A FIDUCIARY DUTY TO PLAINTIFF. DEFENDANT'S DEMURRER TO THE 5TH AND 6TH CAUSES OF ACTION ARE SUSTAINED WITH LEAVE TO AMEND TO SPECIFICALLY SHOW THAT PLAINTIFF AND DEFENDANT WERE IN A BUSINESS RELATIONSHIP OR THAT MONEY WAS TRANSACTED. DEFENDANT'S DEMURRER TO THE 7TH CAUSE OF ACTION IS SUSTAINED WITH LEAVE TO AMEND TO SPECIFY WHAT TYPE OF UCL VIOLATION OCCURRED. DEFENDANT'S DEMURRER TO THE 8TH CAUSE OF ACTION SUSTAINED WITH LEAVE TO AMEND TO ALLEGE A SPECIFIC ACT OF FINANCIAL ELDER ABUSE. DEFENDANT'S DEMURRER TO 9TH CAUSE OF ACTION SUSTAINED WITH LEAVE TO AMEND TO SPECIFICALLY IDENTIFY A CONSPIRACY. PREVAILING PARTY TO SUBMIT A PROPOSED FORM OF ORDER. JUDGE: LORETTA M. GIORGI, REPORTER: KARLA ELLIS-DAVIS, CSR #12998, (302)

DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jan-26-2011 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT WEISS & COMPANY, LLP'S DEMURRER TO FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT. Appearances: John S. Blackman of Farbstein & Blackman (650.554.6200) for defendant and moving party; Charles Murray (990.6696) for plaintiff and responding party. Argued and the court modified its ruling as follows: Defendant's demurrer to 1st and 2nd causes of action are sustained with leave to amend to plead with more specificity where duty lies or specific facts of negligence. Defendant's demurrer to the 3rd cause of action is sustained with leave to amend to plead specific facts showing whether the contract was written, oral or implied conduct per CCP 430.10(g). Defendant's demurrer to the 4th cause of action is sustained with leave to amend to establish that defendant had a fiduciary duty to plaintiff.
Defendant's demurrer to the 5th and 6th causes of action are sustained with leave to amend to specifically show that plaintiff and defendant were in a business relationship or that money was transacted. Defendant's demurrer to the 7th cause of action is sustained with leave to amend to specify what type of UCL violation occurred. Defendant's demurrer to the 8th cause of action sustained with leave to amend to allege a specific act of financial elder abuse.
Defendant's demurrer to 9th cause of action sustained with leave to amend to specifically identify a conspiracy. Prevailing party to submit a proposed form of order. (302/LMG) Judge: Loretta M. Giorgi, Reporter: Karla Ellis-Davis, CSR #12998

MASTER CALENDAR JURY

MOTION And Notice Of Motion For Entry Of Judgment

MINI MINUTES:
DEFENDANT WEISS & COMPANY, LLP'S MOTION FOR ENTRY OF JUDGMENT.

RULING: No appearance on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling - Denied. Plaintiff's request for costs and fees is denied. (302/CWW) JUDGE: CHARLOTTE WALTER WOOLARD, REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR #8600

MISCELLANEOUS ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Nov-18-2010 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Order to Show Cause to Plaintiff's Counsel for failure to comply with San Francisco Superior Court Early Settlement Program Policies and Procedures No. 4. (Administrative fee) is ordered off calendar and dissolved/Fee paid. Proceedings not reported. (212)

Notice And Motion For Judgment On The Pleadings
Off Calendar Sep-09-2010 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: No appearances on this matter. The court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: DEFENDANT WEISS & COMPANY, LLP'S MOTION FOR JUDGMENT ON THE PLEADINGS is granted with leave to amend as to both the 1st and 2nd causes of action. For the 1st cause of action, plaintiff must plead facts showing he was a 3rd party beneficiary of the engagement contract between mot and defendant. As to 2nd cause of action, plaintiff must allege actions, as distinguished from unspoken and unrecorded thoughts and decisions, that would indicate that plaintiff actually relied on the misrepresentations. See Small v. Fritz Companies, Inc. (2003) 30 Cal.4th 167, 184. Prevailing party to submit a proposed form of order. (302/CWW) Judge: Charlotte Walter Woolard, Reporter: Kent Gubbine, CSR #5797
